failure_modes.text: False confidence outside the perimeter can hide unknown unknowns until the decision is already exposed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Map the boundary explicitly and require external validation before committing to decisions outside it; unknown unknowns live just past the perimeter.

failure_modes.text: Treating the circle as a permanent identity label can turn expertise into stagnation instead of a boundary that updates with evidence.
failure_modes.source_quote: "stay in your circle" becomes an excuse to avoid adjacent learning, partnerships, or evidence that the current edge is no longer real.
failure_modes.mitigation: Treat the circle as a living boundary that updates with evidence and practice, not a permanent identity label.

failure_modes.text: Maslow's hammer can make practitioners force familiar tools onto problems that sit outside their real competence.
failure_modes.source_quote: Maslow’s Hammer (The Law of the Instrument)
failure_modes.mitigation: When the problem feels familiar but sits outside your real competence, bring in a practitioner from the relevant domain instead of stretching the familiar tool.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
Is the worst case bad enough?
What variables matter in this situation and why?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What variables matter in this situation and why?"

heuristics.text: Define the real perimeter by identifying what you understand and what you do not understand.
heuristics.source_quote: You have to know what you understand and what you don’t understand.

heuristics.text: Break oversized scopes into bite-size chunks that can actually be solved within current capability and resources.
heuristics.source_quote: the first action is to break it down into "bite-size chunks" that can realistically be solved within the allotted time and resources

heuristics.text: Use plain-English explanation as a perimeter test: if you cannot explain the concept simply, the circle is thinner than you think.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally strong on perimeter discipline and self-calibration, so the chunk set stays close to those operational moves.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style self-branding or talent-positioning overlays because the raw file is stronger on competence-boundary errors and mitigations.
open_questions: The third heuristic is a conservative operational compression of the ally section rather than a standalone raw imperative sentence.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on metacognition, Feynman technique, and multidisciplinary expansion for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section explicitly names Dunning-Kruger, curse of knowledge, and confirmation bias as future relation material.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against authority-bias, commitment-bias, and confirmation-bias.
